items.title: Why it's about to get so much harder to find an aged care bed in WA

children.content: It's going to get much harder to find an available aged care bed in WA, a new report finds, with a "substantial and time-critical shortfall" predicted.

gallery.altText: A close-up photo of an elderly woman knitting.
Simone stands with a neutral expression in an office hallway.
A bed in a resident's room in an aged care home.

items.title: Notorious Perth paedophile Bradley Pen Dragon released from jail again

children.content: A notorious Perth paedophile who breached his prison release conditions 11 times has again been freed into the community, as the child protection minister argues it may be unconstitutional to keep him in custody.

image.altText: onvicted Australian paedophile Bradley Pendragon

items.title: Should bore water be sold as spring water?

children.content: A resident of the Perth Hills, where thousands of litres of groundwater are extracted each week and sold as bottled water, has questioned whether it's accurate to label it as 'spring water'.

image.altText: Bottled water stacked on fridge shelves.

items.title: Man, 23, charged with murder over death of eight-week-old baby

children.content: A 23-year-old man has been charged with murder over the death of an eight-week-old child in suburban Perth.

image.altText: A door of a police car with police tape tied to the side mirror.

items.title: Alleged hit-and-run driver gave himself up after freeway death, police say

children.content: A 22-year-old man dies after being struck by a car on the Mitchell Freeway in Perth's north, with the alleged driver charged and due to face court after he later went to a police station.

image.altText: A wide shot of an empty strethc of the Mitchell Freeway after a fatla crash, with several vehicles on either side of the road.

items.title: Perth's Cottesloe revamp to go ahead but warning more funding needed

children.content: An oceanfront redevelopment at Perth's iconic Cottesloe Beach will go ahead with $15 million in funding, but the mayor says more financial assistance is needed from the state government to complete the project.

gallery.altText: Two women with one wearing hijab siting up and a couple next to them lying on the lawn by beach.

A car park sign by Cottesloe Beach saying "3 hour parking limit at all times".

items.title: Black mould and broken toilets: Inside 'dismal' government-owned homes

children.content: Bug infestations, walls covered in black mould and broken toilets are just some of the issues plaguing homes in Australia's most isolated communities, where the landlord is the state government.

items.title: Exhibition sheds fresh light on WA's pre-colonial history

children.content: Oral histories from Menang Noongar elders are combined with AI renderings of life before colonisation in an exhibition at the WA State Library.

items.title: How a container of hot chocolate put the spotlight on airport security

children.content: A security scare that shut down a Victorian airport last week was found to be a false alarm.

image.altText: Passengers line up for security screening at the airport.

items.title: Travis Head and wife Jess cop social media abuse after IPL on-field incident

children.content: Australian cricketer Travis Head and his wife Jess Head have their Instagram accounts blasted with vile comments after a series of tense on-field exchanges between Head and Indian star Virat Kohli in an Indian Premier League match.

image.altText: Australian batsman Travis head looks at his bat while Indian fielder Virat Kohli smiles and shouts.

items.title: WA health official wants more support to stem 'rare' diphtheria outbreak

children.content: The director of the Communicable Disease Control Directorate says vaccines help prevent more serious symptoms, but more efforts are needed to tackle the diphtheria outbreak in remote areas.

image.altText: A nurse preparing a boostrix vaccination, the vaccine's white and purple box is visible in the foreground.

items.title: The scale of the mouse plague is hard to fathom. Here's a visualisation

children.content: A mouse plague is invading farms and homes in southern and Western Australia. But what does that really look like?

items.title: ASX 200 rises after Dow closes at record high, oil falls under $US100 — as it happened

children.content: The Australian share market has begun the new week in positive territory after the Dow Jones posted new records. Meanwhile, the price of Brent crude has dropped under $US100 a barrel. Look back on our business blog.

items.title: 'It's atrocious, the apathy': Survivor calls on governments to act

children.content: At nine years old, Bronwyn was sent to a notorious government-run children's home without her parents' consent. What unfolded next impacted the rest of her life.

items.title: The migration maths behind the housing crisis is not what politicians claim

children.content: Arthur Calwell argued migration was essential to national survival. Today, the Coalition says it should depend on how many homes get built, but the economics are far from straightforward.

items.title: Trump's push for a ceasefire is good for peace but our wallets still feel the pain

children.content: US President Donald Trump was yesterday touting an end to the war in Iran, but declaring mission accomplished in Washington will not spare Australian consumers, and politicians, from the economic fallout of the conflict.

items.title: Link between terrorism and DV could be key to preventing homicide

children.content: A world-first research project is aiming to prevent family and intimate partner homicides with similar strategies to those being used to tackle terrorism and lone-actor attacks.

items.title: Psychiatrist repeatedly harassed nurse on messaging platforms

children.content: The only psychiatrist in a regional WA area is given a more lenient sanction for sexually harassing a nurse, partially due to a lack of other mental health specialists in the area.

items.title: Albany's hidden WWII fuel tanks transformed into global street art gallery
